shots.title: Dashboard — pinned services with one-click start/stop
shots.blurb: Pin any asset to the dashboard; Start/Stop drives kubectl apply/delete on the runtime closure.

callouts.label: Start — kubectl apply for the asset and every runtime dependency, then runs lifecycle hooks.
Stop — kubectl delete for the same closure.
Pin/unpin: pinned assets persist on the dashboard even when stopped.
Live status chip — turns green when any endpoint of the asset is reachable.
Web UI quick-link — promoted from manifest endpoints with role: "Web UI".
Auto-detected Swagger / OpenAPI URL — probes /v3/api-docs and friends and caches per-host.
Credential fixtures — quick-link to a fixture that surfaces seeded test accounts.
Hide off — collapses stopped cards out of view; turn back on with one click.

shots.title: Assets — search, filter, favorites, ratings
shots.blurb: Catalog of every registered asset. Multi-term search defaults to OR; "all of" forces AND.

callouts.label: Top favorites strip — pinned 5-star favorites surface first so they're always one click away.
Multi-term search box — "all of …" forces AND across name/description/tags/owner/language/repo.
Type filter — narrow to libraries, services, infra, etc.
Favorites-only toggle.
Per-asset row: ♥ favorite + ★★★★★ rating + tags + lifecycle chip + repo description.

shots.title: Global search — assets and full-text doc hits
shots.blurb: The header search drops down with asset matches plus full-text hits inside every workspace's .md files.

callouts.label: Multi-term query — typing redis matches across asset metadata + docs simultaneously.
Asset matches — id / name / description / tags / language / owner / repo url.
Doc hits — full-text scan of every workspace's markdown with line-number snippets and the matching term highlighted.
Tab strip — overview, dependencies, graph, builds, runtime, cluster, analyze, changes, fixtures, docs, panels.
Favorite toggle — boosts the asset to the Top Favorites strip on the assets page.
5-star rating — sortable + filterable across the whole catalog.
Pin to dashboard — adds this asset to the running-services dashboard so you can start/stop it from there.
Ask Claude — opens an annotated context dump tailored to this asset (manifest, deps, recent commits…).
Tag editor with autocomplete — shared tag namespace across the catalog.

shots.title: Panels tab — at-a-glance asset dossier
shots.blurb: Auto-generated panels (GitHub, manifest, endpoints, health, audit, recent commits …) — opt-in info, not a tab cliff.

callouts.label: GitHub panel — repo URL, default branch, owner, lifecycle, derived from the registered remote.
Workspace panel — JGit-managed local checkout + sync state.
Manifest panel — devportal.yaml summary (id, type, language, exposed ports, fixtures…).
Health checks — every host-reachable URL probed in parallel with the latency printed.
Quick actions — direct deep-links to every Web UI / API / WebSocket the manifest declares.
Recent activity — last n commits across the workspace.
Endpoints / detected services — runtime ports surfaced as live URLs.
Audit panel — manifest validation findings + lifecycle/runtime warnings.
Discovered sources — ports, services, env vars the analyzer found in the actual code.

shots.title: Dependency graph — producers and consumers
shots.blurb: Interactive React Flow graph; auto-laid-out via dagre, with edge labels for the dependency kind.

callouts.label: Direction filter — producers, consumers, or both.
Producer depth — limit transitive walk (0 = root only, -1 = unlimited).
Consumer depth — separate cap so you can include direct callers without exploding the graph.
Layout direction — producers right (LR) or producers down (TB).
Fullscreen — pop the canvas out for very large graphs.
ReactFlow controls — zoom in/out, fit-to-view, lock pan.
Nodes coloured by dependency kind; edge labels ("build", "runtime", "data"…) come straight from the manifest.

shots.title: Test fixtures — surfaced credentials and seed data
shots.blurb: Fixtures emit a structured DEVPORTAL_FIXTURE: line so the portal can extract usernames, passwords, login URLs and render them in the UI.

callouts.label: Fixture name + description from devportal.yaml.
Run / Run again — invokes the script in the asset workspace and streams its log.
Run status — exit code, duration, timestamp, full log behind "Show log".
Parsed credentials table — DEVPORTAL_FIXTURE: contract emits username/password/role/url tuples.
Quick-launch links — open the right URL signed in as the right user in one click.

shots.title: Docs tab — markdown with rendered Mermaid
shots.blurb: Every .md in the asset workspace is browseable; mermaid blocks render to SVG inline (lazy-loaded so the bundle stays small).

callouts.label: File tree — every .md found in the asset's local workspace, sorted by path.
Active doc highlighted — click any file to load it.
Raw — link to the original markdown for copy/pasting back to GitHub.
Mermaid diagram rendered to SVG inline — mermaid is dynamic-imported on demand so the docs page only pays for it when needed.
Multiple diagrams per doc render concurrently; the renderer falls back to a styled error block on bad syntax.

shots.title: Runtime tab — ports, docker images, kubectl
shots.blurb: All runtime surfaces in one view: the port registry, local docker images, and kubectl apply targets.

callouts.label: Port reservations — registry-managed slots so two assets never collide.
Allocate local — reserves a host port from the 18000-18999 range.
Allocate NodePort — reserves a slot from 30000-32767 for kubectl exposure.
Release — frees the slot back to the registry.
Docker images section — shows running containers and their status.
+ dependents — apply the runtime closure (this asset and every transitive runtime producer).

shots.title: Port registry — every port the portal knows about
shots.blurb: Three sources unified: local docker host ports, Kubernetes NodePorts, and active kubectl port-forward tunnels.

callouts.label: Single registry across the whole portal — no two assets get the same port.
Filter — by asset id, port, URL, or pod name.
Group toggle — by scope (local/nodeport/forward) or by asset.
Local docker section — host ports backing local containers.
Kubernetes NodePort section — Rancher Desktop forwards these straight to localhost.
Click-to-copy URLs make it trivial to paste a NodePort URL into Postman / curl / browser.
